title: Non-uniqueness of weak solutions to the Navier-Stokes equations in R^3

def:1.1.summary: Weak solution of NSE on $R^3$: u $\in$ C([0,T); $L^2(R^3))$ satisfying the distributional form of NSE against all divergence-free test functions in $C^\infty_0$.

thm:1.2.summary: MAIN RESULT: Non-uniqueness in $C_t L^2(R^3)$. For any two energy profiles e(t) = ẽ(t) on [0, T/2], there exist weak solutions u, ũ with identical initial data but ‖u(t)‖$^2 = e(t) \ne$ ẽ(t) = ‖ũ(t)‖^2 for t > T/2. Extends Buckmaster-Vicol $(T^3)$ to whole space $R^3$.

cor:1.3.summary: Infinitely many weak solutions in $C_t L^2(R^3)$ that dissipate kinetic energy; follows from thm:1.2 by choosing e(t) decreasing and ẽ(t) = const after T/2.

thm:1.4.summary: Application I: Non-uniqueness in smooth bounded domain D. Infinitely many energy-dissipating weak solutions in C([0,T]; $L^2(D))$.

thm:1.5.summary: Application II: Couette flow instability. For any small $\varepsilon > 0$, there is a weak solution $u^\varepsilon$ with ‖$u^\varepsilon(0) -$ U‖ $\le \varepsilon$ that departs distance $\varepsilon^{-1/2}$ from Couette flow U for t $\in [3\varepsilon^{1/2}, 5\varepsilon^{1/2}]$.

prop:2.1.summary: Main iteration step: given $(u_q, p_q$, R̊_q) satisfying inductive estimates at level q, there exists $(u_{q+1}, p_{q+1}$, R̊_{q+1}) satisfying the same estimates at level q+1 with ‖$u_{q+1} - u_q$‖$_{L^\infty_t L^2} \le C_0 \delta_{q+1}^{1/2}$. One convex integration step on $R^3$.

prop:2.2.summary: Branching proposition: if two sequences $(u_q$, R̊_q) and (ũ_q, R̊̃_q) agree (loc and non-loc parts) on [0, $T/4 + \lambda^{-1}_q]$, then their next iterates agree on [0, $T/4 + \lambda^{-1}_{q+1}]$. This shrinking agreement interval is how distinct energy profiles diverge after T/2.

prop:3.1.summary: Mollification estimates: bounds on $(u^{loc}_{$ℓ$_q} - u^{loc}_q, u^{non-loc}_{$ℓ$_q} - u^{non-loc}_q$, R̊_{ℓ_q}, $R^{rem}_q)$ in $H^4, W^{N+5,1}$, and $L^1$ norms. Ensures mollified data controls the energy gap $\delta_{q+1}/2 \lee(t) - \int|u_{ℓ_q}|^2 \le 4\delta_{q+1}$.

prop:3.2.summary: Estimates for amplitude modulator $(\chi_q \rho_q)^{1/2}$ and $(\chi_q \rho_q)^{-1}$ in $H^N$ norms. These encode the energy gap into the perturbation amplitude. Bound: ‖$(\chi_q \rho_q)^{1/2}$‖$_{H^N}$ ≲ $\delta^{-N+1/2}_{q+1} \lambda^{10N+2N\alpha}_q$ ℓ^{-N}_q.

prop:3.3.summary: Estimates for amplitude functions $a_{(k,q)}$: ‖$a_{(k,q)}$‖$_{H^N}$ ≲ ℓ^{-2N}_q and ‖$\partial_t a_{(k,q)}$‖$_{H^N}$ ≲ ℓ^{-6N}_q. These amplitudes select the Mikado wave direction k and encode R̊ via the geometric lemma.

prop:3.4.summary: Space-time estimates for localized building blocks $\varphi_{k,k$̄,k̄̄,$\sigma}$: ‖$D^n_t D^m_x \varphi$‖$_{L^\infty_t L^p(T^3)}$ ≲ $N^{m+n}_\Lambda \lambda^{13m/16 + 33n/16 - 9(1/p - 1/2)/4}_{q+1}$. Controls the Mikado wave packet oscillation in the periodic cell, transferred to $R^3$ via localized cutoff.

prop:3.5.summary: Principal Reynolds stress decomposition: $div(w^{(p)}_{q+1}$ ⊗̊ $w^{(p)}_{q+1}) +$ div R̊_{ℓ_q} = div R̊$^{(1)}_{q+1} - \partial_t w^{(t)}_{q+1} + F^{(1)}_{q+1} + \nablaP^{(1)}_{q+1}$. The new stress R̊^{(1)} satisfies ‖R̊^{(1)}‖$_{L^1}$ ≲ ℓ^{-8}_q $\lambda^{-3/16}_{q+1}$: cancels the old stress while introducing only a small new one. Core step of convex integration.

prop:3.6.summary: Temporal correction decomposition: $\partial_t(w^{(p)}_{q+1} + w^{(c)}_{q+1}) =$ div R̊$^{(2)}_{q+1} + \nablaP^{(2)}_{q+1} + F^{(2)}_{q+1}$. Controls time-derivative errors from the principal perturbation; R̊^{(2)} is localized to $\Omega_{q+1}$ and satisfies ‖R̊^{(2)}‖$_{L^1}$ ≲ ℓ^{-12}_q $\lambda^{-1/16}_{q+1}$.

prop:3.7.summary: Combined error $F_{q+1} = F^{(1)}_{q+1} + F^{(2)}_{q+1}$ satisfies ‖$F_{q+1}$‖$_{H^4} \le \lambda^5_{q+1}$ and ‖$F_{q+1}$‖_{Ẽ$^\infty_t B^{-3/2}_{2,1}}$ ≲ $\lambda^{-40}_q$. The $B^{-3/2}$ norm being small is what makes the non-stationary correction $w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$ small.

prop:3.8.summary: Non-stationary correction $w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$: the unique new ingredient for $R^3$. Solves linearized NSE (eq:LNS) with forcing $F_{q+1}$ and background v $= u_{ℓ_q}$. Bounds: ‖$w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$‖$_{B^{1/2}_{2,1}} \le \lambda^{-20}_q (very$ small). This localized corrector balances compact support of Reynolds stress error against non-compact support of the non-local part of the solution.

prop:3.9.summary: Full perturbation estimates: $w_{q+1} = w^{(p)} + w^{(c)} + w^{(t)} + w^{(tc)} + w^{(ns)}$ satisfies ‖$w_{q+1}$‖$_{L^2} \le C_0 \delta^{1/2}_{q+1}/2$ and ‖$w_{q+1}$‖$_{H^5} \le \lambda^6_{q+1} C_0 \delta^{1/2}_{q+1}/2$. Each component (principal, corrector, temporal, t-corrector, non-stationary) bounded separately.

prop:3.10.summary: New Reynolds stress estimate: R̊_{q+1} defined as sum of R̊^{(1)}, R̊^{(2)}, and remainder terms satisfies ‖R̊_{q+1}‖$_{L^1} \le \delta_{q+2} \lambda^{-4\alpha}_{q+1}$ and ‖R̊_{q+1}‖$_{W^{5,1}} \le \lambda^7_{q+1}$. This closes the inductive hypothesis at level q+1 — the hardest estimate to close in the whole paper.

prop:3.11.summary: Energy gap estimate: $|e(t) - \int|u_{q+1}|^2 - 2\delta_{q+2}| \le \delta_{q+2} \lambda^{-\alpha}_q$. Ensures the energy tracks e(t) precisely at level q+1, with room for the next perturbation $\delta_{q+2}$.

prop:5.1.summary: Bounded domain analogue of thm:1.2: non-uniqueness for NSE on smooth bounded domain D $\subset R^3$ in $C_t L^2(D)$. Proved via the same convex integration scheme adapted to D using Dirichlet boundary conditions.

prop:5.2.summary: Iteration step for bounded domain: given $(v_q, p_q$, R̊_q) on D $\times (0, T_q]$, produces $(v_{q+1}, p_{q+1}$, R̊_{q+1}) on D $\times (0, T_{q+1}]$ with ‖$v_{q+1} - v_q$‖$_{L^2(D)} \le C_0 \delta^{1/2}_{q+1}$. Analogous to prop:2.1.

prop:5.3.summary: Branching proposition for bounded domain: two sequences agreeing in $(v^{loc}_q, v^D_q$, R̊_q) on [0, $T/4 + \lambda^{-1}_q]$ produce next iterates agreeing on [0, $T/4 + \lambda^{-1}_{q+1}]$. Analogous to prop:2.2.

prop:5.4.summary: Couette flow instability: for $\varepsilon > 0$ small, there exists a weak solution $v^\varepsilon$ of the perturbation-from-Couette system with ‖$v^\varepsilon(0)$‖$_{L^2} \le \varepsilon$ but ‖$v^\varepsilon(t)$‖$_{L^2} \ge \varepsilon^{-1/4}$ for t $\in [3\varepsilon^{1/2}, 5\varepsilon^{1/2}]$. Shows $L^2$ instability of Couette flow in spite of linear stability above threshold.

prop:5.5.summary: Iteration step for Couette perturbation system: given $(v_q, P_q$, R̊_q) solving the Couette-minus-Couette system (eq:5.16) at level q, produces level q+1 with ‖$v_{q+1} - v_q$‖$_{L^2} \le C_0 \delta^{1/2}_{q+1}$.

lem:A.1.summary: Geometric lemma (from Beekie-Buckmaster-Vicol): any positive definite symmetric matrix R near Id decomposes as R $= \Sigma_{k \in \Lambda} a^2_k(R)$ k̄ ⊗ k̄ for rational unit vectors k̄. Enables the convex integration construction to express R̊ as a sum of rank-one terms using Mikado waves.

rem:A.2.summary: Explicit example of the direction set $\Lambda$ in lem:A.1 using 6 vectors from Pythagorean triples (5/13, 12/13), with explicit orthonormal bases (k, k̄, k̄̄).

def:A.3.summary: Standard temporal $(\varphi)$ and spatial $(\psi)$ mollifier kernels with unit mass. Used to define the mollified sequence $u_{ℓ_q} = u_q * \varphi_{$ℓ$_q} * \psi_{$ℓ_q}.

lem:A.4.summary: $L^p$ localization lemma (Cheskidov-Modena-Szekely): for compactly supported f on $\Omega \subset [-1/2,1/2]^3$ and $\lambda-periodic$ g, ‖f $\cdotg(\lambda\cdot)$‖$_{L^p} \approx$ ‖f‖$_{L^p}$‖g‖$_{L^p}$ up to error $\lambda^{-1/p}$ ‖f‖$_{C^1}$ ‖g‖$_{L^p}$. Allows Mikado waves on $T^3$ to approximate their $R^3$ analogues.

lem:A.5.summary: Inverse divergence iteration (from BMNV): $G^i \rho^{(0)}(\lambdax) = \partial_j$ R̊$^{ij}_{(N)} + \partial_i P_{(N)} + E^i_{(N)}$ with ‖$E_{(N)}$‖ $= O(\lambda^{-N})$. Decomposes a product into traceless symmetric stress + pressure + error of any desired order. Key tool for constructing $w^{(p)}$ with controlled Reynolds stress support.

def:A.6.summary: Mixed time-space Besov space L̃$^r_T B^s_{p,q}(R^d)$ via Chemin-Lerner norms: ‖u‖ = ‖(2^{js} ‖$\Delta_j$ u‖$_{L^r_t L^p})_{j}$‖_{ℓ^q}. Used for the Besov-type estimates on $w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$ and $F_{q+1}$.

lem:A.7.summary: Maximal regularity for heat equation in Besov spaces (from Bahouri-Chemin-Danchin): if $u_0 \in B^s_{p,q}$ and $\partial_t$ u $- \Deltau =$ f $\in$ L̃$^{r_2}_t B^{s-2+2/r_2}_{p,q}$, then u $\in$ L̃$^{r_1}_t B^{s+2/r_1}_{p,q}$ with natural bound. Used to solve eq:LNS for $w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$.

prop:A.8.summary: Local well-posedness for perturbed Couette NSE (eq:A.3): if ‖$u_0$‖$_{B^{1/2}_{2,1}} +$ ‖v‖_{L̃$^\infty B^{1/2}_{2,1}} +$ ‖f‖_{L̃$^\infty B^{-3/2}_{2,1}} \le \varepsilon$, then solution u exists on [0,T] with T $\le 1/4$. Used in prop:5.5 to close the Couette iteration.

edges.note: Main result is stated in the class of weak solutions defined by def:1.1
Proved by iterating prop:2.1 from q=0 (trivial zero solution) to q $\to \infty$, then taking limit in $L^2$
Non-uniqueness follows from prop:2.2: two starting sequences with e $\ne$ ẽ on [T/2, T] agree on initial interval, then diverge — giving distinct limits with same initial data
Corollary follows by choosing e(t) strictly decreasing and ẽ(t) = e(T/2) for t > T/2
thm:1.4 follows from prop:5.1 exactly as thm:1.2 follows from prop:2.1 + prop:2.2
Couette instability thm:1.5 is exactly prop:5.4 restated in terms of $u^\varepsilon =$ U $+ v^\varepsilon (U =$ Couette flow)
Reynolds stress at level q+1 must satisfy inductive bound — prop:3.10 is the estimate that closes this
Energy gap must track e(t) at level q+1 — prop:3.11 provides this
Perturbation $w_{q+1}$ must satisfy $L^2$ and $H^5$ bounds at level q+1 — prop:3.9 provides these
Mollification estimates control starting point for each iteration step
R̊_{q+1} is assembled from R̊^{(1)} (from prop:3.5), R̊^{(2)} (from prop:3.6), and linear NSE error (from prop:3.8)
R̊^{(2)} contributes the temporal correction part to R̊_{q+1}
The non-stationary correction $w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$ contributes additional Reynolds stress terms from the nonlinear NSE interaction
$w^{(p)}_{q+1}$ bounds in prop:3.9 use the decomposition and estimates from prop:3.5
$w^{(c)}_{q+1}$ and $w^{(t)}_{q+1}$ bounds use the temporal decomposition from prop:3.6
$w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$ bound from prop:3.8 enters the total perturbation estimate
Energy gap $e(t) - \int|u_{q+1}|^2$ computed using the $L^2$ bounds on $w_{q+1}$ from prop:3.9
Mollified energy gap (prop:3.1 eq:3.14) is the starting point for the energy gap at q+1
Geometric lemma decomposes R̊_{ℓ_q} into rank-one components $\Sigma_k a^2_{(k,q)}$ k̄ ⊗ k̄, enabling construction of $w^{(p)}_{q+1}$ via Mikado waves
Inverse divergence iteration converts G $* \rho^{(0)}(\lambdax)$ into compact-support Reynolds stress R̊^{(1)}, which is the key $R^3$ adaptation vs the $T^3$ case
Amplitude estimates for $a_{(k,q)}$ control the $H^N$ norms needed to bound R̊^{(1)} in prop:3.5
Building block estimates for $\varphi_{k,k$̄,k̄̄,$\sigma}$ control the oscillating perturbation in space-time
Temporal decomposition $\partial_t(w^{(p)} + w^{(c)})$ reuses the inverse divergence mechanism from prop:3.5 for the time-derivative error
Inverse divergence applied again to handle $\partial_t w^{(p)}_{q+1}$ contribution to temporal Reynolds stress R̊^{(2)}

$a_{(k,q)}$ are defined as $a_k((\chi_q \rho_q)$ R̊_{ℓ$_q} / \delta_{q+1})$ via lem:A.1; bounds on $(\chi_q \rho_q)^{1/2}$ and $(\chi_q \rho_q)^{-1}$ from prop:3.2 feed directly into $H^N$ estimates for $a_{(k,q)}$
$\rho_q$ derived from mollified Reynolds stress; $L^1$ and $W^{N,1}$ bounds from prop:3.1 give $L^\infty H^N$ control of $(\chi_q \rho_q)$
$L^p$ localization lemma controls ‖f $\cdot \varphi(\lambda\cdot)$‖$_{L^p}$ for the building block functions, converting $T^3$ estimates to $R^3$ with compact support
$F_{q+1}$ includes $F^{(1)}_{q+1}$ from prop:3.5; the $B^{-3/2}_{2,1}$ bound is the key output
$F_{q+1}$ includes $F^{(2)}_{q+1}$ from prop:3.6
$w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$ solves linearized NSE (heat equation + lower-order terms); lem:A.7 provides Besov-space maximal regularity estimate that gives ‖$w^{(ns)}$‖$_{B^{1/2}_{2,1}} \le \lambda^{-20}_q$
$F_{q+1}$ is the forcing for eq:LNS; ‖$F_{q+1}$‖$_{B^{-3/2}_{2,1}}$ ≲ $\lambda^{-40}_q$ being small is what makes $w^{(ns)}$ negligible
Maximal regularity estimate stated in the Chemin-Lerner Besov norms defined by def:A.6
Non-uniqueness in bounded domain proved by iterating prop:5.2 (domain-adapted convex integration step)
Branching at q $\to \infty$ limit uses prop:5.3 to propagate initial agreement into distinct limits
Couette instability built via iterated prop:5.5 (iteration step for Couette-perturbation system)
Local WP for Couette perturbation (prop:A.8) is used to close the iteration at each step when $L^2$ norm is small
prop:A.8 provides existence of the next iterate for the Couette perturbation system (eq:5.16)
Local WP proof uses Besov-space regularity for heat-type equations (linearized Couette NSE is heat equation + drift + nonlinear terms)
Well-posedness estimate for prop:A.8 stated in $B^{1/2}_{2,1}$ Besov norms
rem:A.2 gives the explicit Pythagorean triple direction set $\Lambda$ implementing lem:A.1

layer_1_main_results.description: The main theorem and its direct corollaries; all proved from the induction propositions in layer 2

layer_2_induction_propositions.description: The two propositions that drive non-uniqueness: one-step iteration (prop:2.1) and branching (prop:2.2); thm:1.2 is proved in sec:2.3 by combining these

layer_3_mollification.description: Regularization of $u_q$ to smooth mollified data $u_{ℓ_q}$; provides starting estimates for the convex integration step

layer_4_amplitude_building_blocks.description: Amplitude modulator construction and localized Mikado wave estimates; feed into the principal perturbation construction

layer_5_perturbation_construction.description: The core convex integration construction on $R^3$: geometric decomposition of R̊ (lem:A.1), inverse divergence to maintain compact support (lem:A.5), assembly of $w^{(p)}$ and Reynolds stress decompositions

layer_6_r3_correction.description: The uniquely $R^3$ ingredient: $w^{(ns)}_{q+1}$ corrects for the non-compact support of the non-local part; requires Besov-space maximal regularity

layer_7_closing_induction.description: Assembles all perturbation estimates to close the inductive hypotheses at level q+1: full $w_{q+1}$ bounds, R̊_{q+1} bound, energy gap

layer_8_bounded_domain_application.description: Parallel convex integration on smooth bounded domain D; same architecture adapted to Dirichlet boundary

layer_9_couette_application.description: Couette flow instability in $L^2(R^3)$; convex integration in the Couette-perturbation system, with local WP to start each step

BV.title: Nonuniqueness of weak solutions to the Navier-Stokes equation
BV.note: Primary predecessor: proved non-uniqueness on $T^3$; this paper extends to $R^3$

ABC.title: Non-uniqueness of Leray solutions of the forced Navier-Stokes equations
ABC.note: Non-uniqueness for forced NSE; related technique

BMNV.title: Non-conservative H^{1/2-} weak solutions of the incompressible 3D Euler equations
BMNV.note: Source of inverse divergence iteration step (lem:A.5)

1Cheskidov.title: Sharp nonuniqueness for the Navier-Stokes equations

2Beekie.title: Weak solutions of ideal MHD which do not conserve magnetic helicity

MoS.title: Non-uniqueness for the transport equation with Sobolev vector fields

BCD11.title: Fourier Analysis and Nonlinear Partial Differential Equations
BCD11.note: Source of Besov space tools: def:A.6 and lem:A.7

MWZ.title: Littlewood-Paley Theory and Applications to Fluid Dynamics Equations

BGM17.title: On the stability threshold for the 3D Couette flow in Sobolev regularity
BGM17.note: Background for Couette flow stability; motivates thm:1.5
